anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge — the embodied is not the agent of the body's acts
anchored_threads.evidence: The verse continues the chapter's first thread: what is invariant under change. The engineering layer maps the symmetric joint negation to the deprecation context — both author and engineer make the same category mistake about what is actually being killed. This anchors directly on the chapter thesis claim that 'the interface survives even when the implementation churns.'

verdict_reason: The verse anchors thread 1 of the chapter and supports the substrate doctrine progressively (16: cut; 17: name; 18: license; 19: negate symmetric attribution; 20: name as ancient). Voice is correct. Hard edge preserved (the falsifiability does not soften deprecation duties). PASS.
